message:
tools: explicit ripgrep termination on match limit to prevent buffering

When grep reaches the match limit, explicitly kill the ripgrep process
rather than just breaking the read loop and waiting for natural exit.

Problem: When ripgrep has unbounded matches (e.g., searching for common
patterns in large codebases), the stdout/stderr channels could buffer
indefinitely while we wait for the process to exit naturally.

Solution:
- Call guard.kill() when match_limit_reached is true
- Drain any remaining buffered stdout/stderr messages
- Return exit code 0 since we intentionally terminated

This prevents memory growth from buffered channel data and ensures
prompt cleanup of subprocess resources.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
